title: Charred Silky German Lamb Kidney Khao Khua
description: A monsoon-inspired German dish featuring lamb kidney, fry-grind-rice-khao-khua, and a silky, charred finish. This keto-friendly recipe is perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of coconut oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the lamb kidney and cook for 3-4 minutes on each side, or until browned.
Remove the lamb kidney from the skillet and set aside.
Reduce the heat to medium and add the remaining 1 tablespoon of coconut oil to the skillet.
Add the onion and cook for 4-5 minutes, or until softened.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute, or until fragrant.
Stir in the flour and cook for 1-2 minutes, or until lightly toasted.
Slowly pour in the lemon juice and honey, whisking constantly.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 2-3 minutes, or until thickened.
Add the browned lamb kidney back to the skillet and stir to coat with the sauce.
Serve the lamb kidney over khao khua, garnished with chopped parsley and red pepper flakes if desired.
Fluff the khao khua with a fork before serving.
